full_text:
Temperature nears 46°C, heatwave alert in 18 districts:Extreme heat expected from today, doctors warn against sun exposure
- Copy link
Rajasthan witnessed light rain on Saturday even as intense heatwave conditions continued across the state. Weather changed in parts of Jaipur, Tonk, Alwar, Hanumangarh, and Sri Ganganagar districts during the afternoon.
Ahead of the Nautapa period, heatwave conditions have become more severe in western Rajasthan. Jaisalmer’s temperature has once again reached close to 46°C.
The Meteorological Centre in Jaipur has predicted severe heat and heatwave conditions across the state for the next 2 to 4 days. Yellow and orange alerts have been issued for several districts.
The state is likely to witness the hottest days of the season. On May 24, an orange alert has been issued for three districts, while 15 districts remain under yellow alert.
Key weather updates
Rain and Hailstorm activity Light rain was reported in parts of Sri Ganganagar and Hanumangarh on Saturday morning. Later in the afternoon, isolated showers occurred in areas of Alwar, Jaipur, Tonk, and Sawai Madhopur. Hailstorms were also reported in some locations near the borders of Tonk, Alwar, and Jaipur districts.
Temperature near 46°C Heatwave conditions intensified during the last 24 hours. Jaisalmer recorded the highest temperature in the state at 45.9°C. Phalodi, Barmer, and Jaisalmer were among the worst-affected areas.
Rising UV index increases health rtisks
Weather expert Dr. RS Devda said most parts of India are currently experiencing severe heatwave conditions. Due to intense sunlight in the coming days, the ultraviolet (UV) index is expected to remain extremely high between 10 am and 5 pm.
Direct exposure to sunlight during this period may cause severe eye irritation and skin burns. Doctors and weather experts have advised people to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours unless absolutely necessary.
Weather Conditions in Major Rajasthan Cities
Jaipur
The city experienced strong sunlight and hot winds till afternoon. Weather changed after 2 pm with cloud cover and dusty winds in several areas. Light drizzle was reported in some rural pockets near Jaipur. The maximum temperature rose by 1°C from Friday to reach 42.6°C, while the minimum temperature settled at 30.8°C.
Udaipur
Heat conditions intensified further in the Lake City. The maximum temperature on Saturday was recorded at 41.9°C, while the minimum temperature stood at 27.5°C. A day earlier, the city had recorded a maximum temperature of 41°C.
Ajmer
Residents faced intense heat and humidity from the morning itself. The maximum temperature was recorded at 41.6°C and the minimum at 29.7°C. Weather experts said there is little possibility of relief from the heat in the coming days.
Sikar
Rainfall was reported in the city and nearby areas. Strong winds had been blowing since late night, and cloudy conditions persisted through the morning. Hot winds were followed by light drizzle in the afternoon. Humidity remained high throughout the day. The maximum temperature increased by 0.5°C to 42.5°C, while the minimum temperature was 25.8°C.
Alwar
The weather changed suddenly on Saturday afternoon as dark clouds covered the sky and rainfall continued for around 15 minutes in several areas. The day temperature was recorded at around 44°C. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for Sunday and Monday, with temperatures likely to touch 46°C over the next two days.
Jodhpur
The city recorded a maximum temperature of 42.8°C and a minimum temperature of 32°C in the last 24 hours. Residents continued to face hot and humid conditions. The weather department has forecast rising heat and humidity over the next seven days.
Kota
The impact of extreme heat is also being seen on wildlife. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 43.8°C on Saturday. To protect animals from the heat, Abheda Biological Park authorities have installed ducting systems and coolers for both herbivorous and carnivorous animals.

title: Heatwave: Taking Toll On Brain, Eye Health; Doctors Advise Caution Amid Soaring Temperatures - The Global Kashmir
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Health experts say soaring temperatures, dehydration and prolonged exposure to sunlight can disturb the body’s internal balance and trigger fatigue, dizziness, severe headaches, migraines, heat exhaustion and even neurological complications in vulnerable individuals.
Hospitals across Delhi-NCR are witnessing a rise in patients reporting heat-related ailments, including eye irritation, dehydration-induced headaches and heat-triggered neurological symptoms, doctors said.
“The ongoing heatwave is beginning to show an impact beyond routine heatrelated illnesses, with a noticeable increase in neurological complaints in our OPD,” Dr Vinit Suri, Senior Consultant- Neurology, Indraprastha Apollo Hospital, Delhi, said.
“We have seen nearly a 20 per cent increase in OPD patients over the past few days, with more people coming in with symptoms such as severe headaches, dizziness, disorientation, fainting episodes, worsening of existing neurological conditions, and especially migraine flare-ups,” he said.
Heat exposure and prolonged exposure to bright sunlight can act as triggers for migraines in some individuals, Dr Suri said. Extreme heat and dehydration can also affect blood flow to the brain, alter electrolyte balance and place significant stress on the nervous system, particularly in vulnerable individuals, he explained.
Symptoms such as persistent confusion, slurred speech, unusual drowsiness, seizures or loss of consciousness should not be ignored, as they may indicate a serious neurological emergency and require immediate medical attention.
Dr Sushma Sharma, Director, Neurology, at Marengo Asia Hospitals, said people suffering from migraine, epilepsy and other neurological disorders may face worsening symptoms during periods of extreme heat and poor sleep caused by hot nights and power cuts.
“During extreme summer heat, it is important to avoid stepping out during peak afternoon hours whenever possible. If going outdoors is necessary, people should protect themselves by using umbrellas, sunglasses, and covering their heads. Staying hydrated is equally important because dehydration caused by excessive sweating can also lead to confusion and neurological complications,” Dr Sharma said.
She advised people to consume natural electrolyte-rich drinks such as coconut water, buttermilk and fresh fruit juices to compensate for fluid loss caused by excessive sweating.
Doctors also cautioned that eye health often gets neglected during summer despite the season significantly increasing the risk of irritation and infections. Dr Neeraj Sanduja, Director at Viaan Eye and Retina Centre, said excessive exposure to sunlight, hot winds, dust and dehydration commonly lead to eye strain and discomfort.
“Summer heat can affect eye health in several ways. Prolonged exposure to strong sunlight, hot winds, dust and dehydration can lead to dryness, irritation, redness and eye strain.
“Many people also experience burning sensation, itching or watery eyes during this season. We also get cases of dry eye syndrome, allergic conjunctivitis and corneal sunburns. Children and those spending long hours outdoors should be extra careful,” Dr Sanduja said.
Dr Namrata Sharma, a professor at the Dr R P Centre for Ophthalmic Sciences, Delhi, said continuous use of air-conditioners and increased screen time indoors are also contributing to eye-related complaints during summer.
“Dry indoor air from air-conditioners, combined with prolonged mobile and laptop use, reduces natural tear production and worsens eye dryness. People often ignore early symptoms such as redness, blurry vision and irritation, which can gradually become more serious if left untreated,” she said.
“We are seeing more cases of allergies, dry eye and infections which happen because of faster evaporation of tear film,” she said.
She advised people to wear UV-protected goggles outdoors, wash their eyes with clean water after exposure to dust, avoid rubbing the eyes with unclean hands, use lubricating drops and maintain adequate hydration throughout the day. (Agencies)

title: PCL chief pushes for uninterrupted power supply amid heatwave - The Times of India
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Lucknow: Amid the ongoing severe heatwave in UP, the state govt is working with full seriousness to ensure uninterrupted power supply as per the predetermined schedule, said officials. Acting on the instructions of chief minister
Yogi Adityanath, the energy department has intensified monitoring across the state to provide relief to residents during the extreme weather.
Ashish Kumar Goel, additional chief secretary (energy) and chairman of UP Power Corporation Limited (UPPCL), reviewed the statewide power supply situation.
He held detailed discussions with MDs of all discoms as well as executive engineers, seeking updates and issuing necessary instructions.
Chief engineers from Noida, Lucknow, Ghaziabad, Bulandshahr, Bijnor, Meerut, Muzaffarnagar, Saharanpur, Moradabad, Rampur, Mathura, Firozabad, Jhansi, Bareilly, Unnao, Varanasi, Ayodhya, Gorakhpur, Deoria and Kanpur (KESCO) provided updates on supply conditions in their regions.
Goel emphasised that uninterrupted electricity is crucial during extreme heat. He instructed officials to ensure power supply strictly according to schedule, with rural areas receiving 18 hours and urban areas 24 hours of electricity. He directed special vigilance at substations and sensitive points, along with night patrolling. Areas witnessing sudden load spikes during the night are to be identified and inspected with vigilance teams.
The UPPCL chairman also reviewed issues related to transformer damage, tripping and availability of materials.
He instructed officials to conduct regular inspections of power lines, substations, and transformers, and to ensure quick resolution of complaints received on the 1912 helpline.
Goel directed that more officers be deployed at sensitive substations and that relay settings be corrected with support from test division teams.
He urged officers to remain present in their respective areas, maintain good public contact, and create WhatsApp groups involving public representatives, media persons, consumers, and social workers for timely sharing of power-related updates.

title: MLAs urge power min to check power cuts amid heatwave - The Times of India
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
Lucknow: Mounting power cuts across UP have triggered political reactions, with legislators from both the
BJP and opposition writing letters and social media posts to state energy minister Arvind Kumar Sharma over the worsening electricity crisis during peak summer.
BJP MLA from Gonda Sadar, Prateek Bhushan Singh, in his letter described a “serious electricity crisis” in his district and constituency. Calling the issue “extremely sensitive and urgent,” Singh said repeated outages amid intense heat had pushed residents into severe distress and disrupted normal life, workplaces and business activity.
He urged the minister to ensure an emergency mechanism for uninterrupted power supply, particularly during night hours, and sought immediate intervention for a permanent solution.
Biswa BJP MLA, Nirmal Verma also wrote to the energy minister, saying both urban and rural areas of his constituency in Sitapur district had been facing continuous scheduled power cuts for several days. He said that the outages had severely affected trade, agriculture and daily life and urged officials to restore normal supply at the earliest.
SP chief
Akhilesh Yadav also attacked the BJP govt over the crisis. In a public appeal, he urged people not to vent anger on linemen and junior electricity employees, claiming they were already working under pressure after large-scale contractual staff layoffs.
He blamed govt for failing to strengthen generation, transmission and distribution systems despite rising demand.
BSP chief
Mayawati also criticised the power situation in UP amid the ongoing heatwave, saying complaints of inadequate electricity supply and frequent outages were making life “extremely distressing” for people across the state.
“Even in a state like UP with its vast population, during this season of severe heat, the common complaints regarding inadequate electricity supply and frequent outages have made life extremely distressing for the poor, middle class, farmers, small traders and millions of other hardworking people,” Mayawati said.
Mayawati appealed to govt to take immediate corrective measures keeping in mind the hardships faced by the public due to electricity-related problems.
Nagina MP Chandra Shekhar Azad too wrote to Sharma, highlighting worsening outages in rural and semi urban areas. He said students, farmers, labourers and small traders were among the worst affected.
In Lucknow, MLAs Neeraj Vora (Lucknow North) and Rajeshwar Singh (Sarojini Nagar) submitted complaints about outages affecting the state capital and adjoining districts, urging the state energy minister to enforce corrective measures.

title: गर्मी का डबल अटैक: दिन में लू और रात में तपिश से लोग परेशान - Dainik Bhaskar
search_term: (लू OR "लू चलना" OR "भीषण गर्मी" OR "प्रचंड गर्मी" OR "कड़ी धूप" OR "तेज धूप" OR "गर्म हवा" OR "उष्ण लहर" OR "ताप लहर" OR "हीट वेव" OR "झुलसाने वाली गर्मी" OR "तपती गर्मी" OR "भयंकर गर्मी" OR "गर्मी का कहर" OR "गर्मी का प्रकोप" OR "अत्यधिक गर्मी" OR मौसम OR "मौसम अपडेट" OR "मौसम का हाल" OR "मौसम पूर्वानुमान" OR "मौसम अलर्ट" OR आईएमडी OR "आईएमडी पूर्वानुमान" OR "आईएमडी अलर्ट" OR "तापमान में बढ़ोतरी" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR "गर्मी से निजात") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
- Double Attack Of Heat: People Are Troubled By Heat Wave During The Day And Heat At Night.
गर्मी का डबल अटैक: दिन में लू और रात में तपिश से लोग परेशान
- कॉपी लिंक
भास्कर न्यूज | झालावाड़
जिले में गर्मी अब चौबीस घंटे परीक्षा लेने लगी है। दिन में तेज धूप और लू के थपेड़ों के बाद अब रात की तपिश भी बेहाल कर रही है। शनिवार को जिले में अधिकतम तापमान 43 डिग्री सेल्सियस दर्ज किया गया, जबकि न्यूनतम तापमान एक डिग्री बढ़कर 31 डिग्री तक पहुंच गया। रात में भी गर्म हवाएं चलने से लोगों को राहत नहीं मिल सकी।
दिनभर चिलचिलाती धूप के कारण बाजारों और सड़कों पर सन्नाटा पसरा रहा। दोपहर के समय लोग जरूरी काम होने पर ही घरों से बाहर निकले। शाम ढलने के बाद लोगों को राहत मिलने की उम्मीद थी, लेकिन रात में भी गर्मी का असर बना रहा। घरों की दीवारों और छतों से निकलती गर्मी ने लोगों की परेशानी बढ़ा दी। शहर के कई इलाकों में लोग देर रात तक घरों की छतों और आंगन में बैठे नजर आए। (तापमान डिग्री सेल्सियस में) दिन 24 मई 25 मई 26 मई 27 मई अस्पताल पहुंच रहे हैं डिहाइड्रेशन के रोगी अस्पतालों में भी गर्मी और डिहाइड्रेशन से जुड़ी शिकायतों वाले मरीज बढ़ने लगे हैं। डॉक्टर लोगों को दोपहर में धूप से बचने, पर्याप्त पानी पीने और जरूरी होने पर ही बाहर निकलने की सलाह दे रहे हैं।
मौसम विभाग ने अगले कुछ दिनों तक गर्मी से राहत मिलने की संभावना कम जताई है। लू से बचने के लिए अपनाएं घरेलू उपाय तेज गर्मी और लू से बचने के लिए लोग घरों में बेल का शरबत, छाछ, नींबू पानी और आम पन्ना जैसे पारंपरिक पेय का सहारा ले रहे हैं। सिर ढककर बाहर निकलना और सूती कपड़े पहनना भी बढ़ गया है। डॉक्टरों ने खाली पेट धूप में बाहर नहीं निकलने और ज्यादा से ज्यादा तरल पदार्थ लेने की सलाह दी है। मौसम केंद्र जयपुर के वैज्ञानिक राधेश्याम शर्मा ने बताया कि रविवार को तापमान एक से दो डिग्री बढ़ने की संभावना है। रात्रि का तापमान भी 30 डिग्री से ऊपर दर्ज होने से ऊष्ण रात्रि रहेगी। एक सप्ताह गर्मी से राहत की उम्मीद नहीं है। अधिकतम 44 44 44 44 न्यूनतम 30 30 29 29 झालावाड़. शहर में शनिवार को दोपहर में तेज धूप ने किया बेहाल।

title: तेज गर्मी में हीट स्ट्रोक और डिहाइड्रेशन से बचाने के लिए बरतें सावधानी - Dainik Bhaskar
search_term: (सूर्याघात OR तापाघात OR "लू लगना" OR "लू से मौत" OR "गर्मी से मौत" OR "हीट स्ट्रोक" OR "सन स्ट्रोक" OR "लू से मरे" OR निर्जलीकरण OR डिहाइड्रेशन OR "अस्पताल में भर्ती" OR "गर्मी से बेहाल" OR "गर्मी से त्राहि-त्राहि") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
full_text:
तेज गर्मी में हीट स्ट्रोक और डिहाइड्रेशन से बचाने के लिए बरतें सावधानी
- कॉपी लिंक
भास्कर न्यूज | लुधियाना शहर में लगातार बढ़ते तापमान का असर अब पालतू जानवरों की सेहत पर भी साफ दिखाई देने लगा है। तेज धूप और गर्म हवाओं के कारण डॉग्स और कैट्स में हीट स्ट्रोक, डिहाइड्रेशन, स्किन एलर्जी और थकान जैसी समस्याएं तेजी से बढ़ रही हैं।
पशु चिकित्सकों के अनुसार गर्मियों में पालतू जानवरों की देखभाल में थोड़ी सी लापरवाही भी उनके लिए गंभीर परेशानी का कारण बन सकती है। खासकर छोटे पपी, ज्यादा उम्र के पालतू जानवर और लंबे बालों वाले डॉग्स इस मौसम में ज्यादा संवेदनशील होते हैं।
डॉक्टरों का कहना है कि कई लोग गर्मी की गंभीरता को नजरअंदाज कर दोपहर के समय पालतू जानवरों को बाहर घुमाने ले जाते हैं या उन्हें बंद और गर्म जगहों पर छोड़ देते हैं। इससे उनकी तबीयत बिगड़ सकती है। यही वजह है कि इन दिनों वेट क्लीनिकों में ऐसे मामलों की संख्या बढ़ने लगी है।
एक्सपर्ट्स का मानना है कि थोड़ी सावधानी और सही देखभाल से पालतू जानवरों को गर्मी के दुष्प्रभाव से सुरक्षित रखा जा सकता है। {स्किन और फर की सफाई जरूरी : गर्मी के मौसम में पालतू जानवरों में पसीना, धूल और बैक्टीरिया के कारण स्किन इंफेक्शन, खुजली और बदबू जैसी समस्याएं बढ़ सकती हैं, इसलिए डॉग्स और कैट्स की नियमित सफाई और ग्रूमिंग बेहद जरूरी होती है।
समय-समय पर उनके फर को ब्रश करें ताकि गंदगी जमा न हो और त्वचा साफ बनी रहे। जरूरत पड़ने पर हल्के और डॉक्टर द्वारा सुझाए गए पेट शैंपू का इस्तेमाल करें। हालांकि बहुत ज्यादा बाल काटने से बचना चाहिए, क्योंकि फर शरीर को तेज गर्मी से भी बचाने में मदद करता है। साफ-सफाई रखने से पालतू जानवर स्वस्थ और आरामदायक महसूस करते हैं।
हर समय साफ पानी उपलब्ध रखें : गर्मी में पालतू जानवरों के शरीर से पानी तेजी से निकलता है। इसलिए उनके पास हमेशा साफ और ताजा पानी होना जरूरी है। पानी की कमी से कमजोरी और डिहाइड्रेशन हो सकता है। {दोपहर में बाहर घुमाने से बचें : दोपहर के समय सड़क और जमीन बहुत ज्यादा गर्म हो जाती है, जिससे डॉग्स के पंजों में जलन और चोट लग सकती है, इसलिए पालतू जानवरों को सुबह जल्दी या शाम के ठंडे समय में ही बाहर घुमाना बेहतर माना जाता है।
इससे वे गर्मी से सुरक्षित रहते हैं। {हीट स्ट्रोक के संकेत पहचानें : अगर पालतू जानवर बहुत ज्यादा हांफने लगे, सुस्त दिखाई दे या बार-बार लेटने लगे तो यह हीट स्ट्रोक का संकेत हो सकता है। ऐसे में तुरंत उसे ठंडी जगह पर रखें और डॉक्टर से संपर्क करें। {कमरे को ठंडा और हवादार रखें : गर्मी में पालतू जानवरों को बंद और गर्म कमरे में रखना खतरनाक साबित हो सकता है।
घर में अच्छी वेंटिलेशन और ठंडी जगह होना जरूरी है, ताकि उन्हें आराम मिल सके। तेज गर्मी से बचाने के लिए उन्हें कभी भी बंद कार या बिना हवा वाली जगह में अकेला न छोड़ें। {हल्का और पौष्टिक भोजन दें : गर्मी में ज्यादा भारी और ऑयली खाना पालतू जानवरों की सेहत बिगाड़ सकता है।
उन्हें ताजा, हल्का और पौष्टिक भोजन देना बेहतर माना जाता है। लंबे समय तक रखा खाना जल्दी खराब हो सकता है, इसलिए हमेशा फ्रेश फूड ही दें ताकि वे स्वस्थ और एक्टिव बने रहें।
